blocks.html_en:
> take up to hours at a time to run, so Claude is « monitoring » tasks most of the tim

How is Claude monitoring them for hours? Claude runs out of context and extremely long sessions are prohibitively expensive even according to Anthropic (after they dispense with the marketing bullshit of long running tasks)?

sentences.en: > take up to hours at a time to run, so Claude is « monitoring » tasks most of the timHow is Claude monitoring them for hours?
sentences.zh: > 一次运行需要几个小时，所以 Claude 大部分时间都在“监视”任务 Claude 如何监视它们几个小时？

sentences.en: Claude runs out of context and extremely long sessions are prohibitively expensive even according to Anthropic (after they dispense with the marketing bullshit of long running tasks)?
sentences.zh: 即使根据 Anthropic 的说法，克劳德断章取义，而且极长的会话成本也高得令人望而却步（在他们放弃了长期运行任务的营销废话之后）？

blocks.html_en: It launches these tasks in the background. It became really good at it a couple of months ago, now it sets monitors on timer (not something I instructed, so I assumed it’s part of the system prompt for this kind of tasks) and then just wait for the next prompt, for the background process to be done, or for a monitor to trigger a checkup.

sentences.en: It launches these tasks in the background.
sentences.zh: 它在后台启动这些任务。

sentences.en: It became really good at it a couple of months ago, now it sets monitors on timer (not something I instructed, so I assumed it’s part of the system prompt for this kind of tasks) and then just wait for the next prompt, for the background process to be done, or for a monitor to trigger a checkup.
sentences.zh: 几个月前它变得非常擅长，现在它在计时器上设置监视器（不是我指示的，所以我假设它是此类任务的系统提示的一部分），然后只需等待下一个提示，完成后台进程，或等待监视器触发检查。

blocks.html_en:
> and then just wait for the next prompt

A single session running for multiple hours is prohibitively expensive, as per Anthropic. Regardless of whether it just waits for a prompt or does something.

sentences.zh: > 然后等待下一个提示 根据 Anthropic 的说法，运行多个小时的单个会话的成本过高。

sentences.en: Regardless of whether it just waits for a prompt or does something.
sentences.zh: 无论它只是等待提示还是执行某些操作。

blocks.html_en: Prohibitively expensive is all relative. Pre-Fable, I was getting fine on the 5x plan for 1-2 concurrent long-running tasks plus interactive work (I do a lot of coding for work, but it is not my full work day). I don’t think a cache miss every hour on Opus hurts that much, even at 500-600k context. It would be nice if they got /clear working on remote-control.

sentences.en: Prohibitively expensive is all relative.
sentences.zh: 极其昂贵都是相对的。

sentences.en: Pre-Fable, I was getting fine on the 5x plan for 1-2 concurrent long-running tasks plus interactive work (I do a lot of coding for work, but it is not my full work day).
sentences.zh: 在《寓言前》中，我对 1-2 个并发长时间运行任务加上交互式工作的 5x 计划感到满意（我为工作编写了大量代码，但这不是我的完整工作日）。

sentences.en: I don’t think a cache miss every hour on Opus hurts that much, even at 500-600k context.
sentences.zh: 我认为 Opus 上每小时的缓存未命中不会造成太大影响，即使在 500-600k 的上下文中也是如此。

sentences.en: It would be nice if they got /clear working on remote-control.
sentences.zh: 如果他们能够 /clear 进行远程控制工作，那就太好了。

blocks.html_en: Is the high expense coming from cache misses? If their workload does need to wait for a long time before it can continue, I wonder would starting new sessions and having to re-read the contexts and results anyway be any cheaper.

sentences.en: Is the high expense coming from cache misses?
sentences.zh: 高额费用是否来自缓存未命中？

sentences.en: If their workload does need to wait for a long time before it can continue, I wonder would starting new sessions and having to re-read the contexts and results anyway be any cheaper.
sentences.zh: 如果他们的工作量确实需要等待很长时间才能继续，我想知道启动新会话并重新读取上下文和结果是否会更便宜。

blocks.html_en: Is the high expense coming from cache misses? If their workload does need to wait for a long time before it can continue, I wonder would starting new sessions and having to re-read the contexts and results anyway be any cheaper.

blocks.html_en:
> Is the high expense coming from cache misses?

Yup. They can't keep your workload in cache forever, or they would run out of cache for users.

> I wonder would starting new sessions and having to re-read the contexts and results anyway be any cheaper.

Yes, that's what they recommend

blocks.html_en:
I've used it for the following when I've had tokens to burn:

  - Fuzzing with the goal for it to apply domain-specific and source-informed knowledge to choose specific fuzzing approaches.
  - More generally, any optimization problem that benefits from domain-specific or source informed knowledge.
  - Running Microsoft's SkillOpt [0].

[0]: https://github.com/microsoft/SkillOpt

blocks.html_en: The other day I used Claude Cowork to create a checklist for me so that I can safely re-install my laptop... It went through all ssh configuration, apps, downloads, documents etc... Once I had the checklist, I asked it to do the backup itself, along with instructions to re-install/setup everything once I had the laptop re-installed. This was something I was procrastinating for too long already...

blocks.html_en:
Few cases I have found very useful myself

1/ Using GUI software. My agents are using headful Google Chrome and Figma. It helps a lot to have separate environment, which is not interfering my main machine.

2/ Running long processes (1h+), so I can leave main machine closed.

3/ Running intensive processes. I use Gemma, Whisper and Qwen, which could burn main machine CPU and resources.

sentences.en: Few cases I have found very useful myself1/ Using GUI software.

sentences.en: My agents are using headful Google Chrome and Figma.
sentences.zh: 我的代理正在使用 Google Chrome 和 Figma。

sentences.en: It helps a lot to have separate environment, which is not interfering my main machine.2/ Running long processes (1h+), so I can leave main machine closed.3/ Running intensive processes.

sentences.en: I use Gemma, Whisper and Qwen, which could burn main machine CPU and resources.
sentences.zh: 我用的是Gemma、Whisper和Qwen，它们会消耗主机CPU和资源。

blocks.html_en:
Running tests that the agent created for its own hallucinations? Optimize using another hallucinating agent?

Which tests and optimizations do you propose to run after a night of supervised work when one of main things that all agents keep doing is "load all records from db , and filter them in memory"? It's now become so bad, I had to literally vibecode a separate linter for this. And that's just one of the problems.

blocks.html_en:
we don't have master AI that can create a great product out of a mediocre prompt.

but we do have sufficient AI to make a great product out of a great prompt.

garbage in -> garbage out hasn't gone anywhere.

so: much like to anyone that blindly complains that their compiler hates them : if you actually want help, provide information. If you just want to complain that the compiler is mean, scream at the sky.

plenty of people have figured out how to get this to work; more than enough to confirm that a straight <gambling-machine>/<hallucinatory-psychopath>/<random-number-generator> analogy is too simplistic to explain what we're working with.

sentences.en: we don't have master AI that can create a great product out of a mediocre prompt.but we do have sufficient AI to make a great product out of a great prompt.garbage in -> garbage out hasn't gone anywhere.so: much like to anyone that blindly complains that their compiler hates them : if you actually want help, provide information.
sentences.zh: 我们没有可以在平庸的提示中创建出出色产品的人工智能大师。但我们确实有足够的人工智能，可以在出色的提示中制作出出色的产品。垃圾输入 -> 垃圾输出并没有消失。所以：就像任何盲目抱怨编译器讨厌他们的人一样：如果您确实需要帮助，请提供信息。

sentences.en: If you just want to complain that the compiler is mean, scream at the sky.plenty of people have figured out how to get this to work; more than enough to confirm that a straight <gambling-machine>/<hallucinatory-psychopath>/<random-number-generator> analogy is too simplistic to explain what we're working with.
sentences.zh: 如果你只是想抱怨编译器很卑鄙，那就对天尖叫吧。很多人已经想出了如何让它工作；足以确认直接的 <gambling-machine>/<hallucinatory-psychopath>/<random-number-generator> 类比过于简单化，无法解释我们正在研究的内容。

blocks.html_en:
Demagoguery. Your "great product" (where is it? show me these great products) will have wildly ineficient code and things like "read the entire DB into memory" regardless of the prompt.

> plenty of people have figured out how to get this to work

Plenty of people claim they have figured it out. In reality these people are full of shit and assume that if LLMs can produce working software, it's great working software. And also assume that LoC is a measure of quality.

Because without fail all the models keep doing this: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48962703

And you can only see that in your "great product" if you actually read the code and understand what's going on.

blocks.html_en:
>It's now become so bad, I had to literally vibecode a separate linter for this.

You see, there's your problem right there. You're vibe coding, which by definition literally means you're unwilling to look at the generated code. That's not what successful ai assisted software developers are doing. YOU HAVE TO READ THE CODE. Refusing to do that means you're not a serious programmer, you're outsourcing your thought and design and implementation, trying to get something for nothing by taking the easy way out, and you're going to get terrible results no matter what prompts you "engineer". There ain't no such thing as a free lunch (yet).

And while we're at it, to elaborate on what serf said: people mindlessly parroting terms like "stochastic parrot" to criticize llms without having read the actual paper that coined the term and understanding what it really claimed and how other papers responded to it means you're just a human stochastic parrot no better than what you're criticizing -- at least the llm has read all those papers and understands what "stochastic parrot" actually means in context. Ask it, it will be glad to explain!

Emily M. Bender, Timnit Gebru, Angelina McMillan-Major, and Margaret Mitchell. On the Dangers of Stochastic Parrots: Can Language Models Be Too Big? (FAccT 2021)

https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3442188.3445922

sentences.en: >It's now become so bad, I had to literally vibecode a separate linter for this.You see, there's your problem right there.
sentences.zh: >现在变得如此糟糕，我不得不为此编写一个单独的 linter。你看，这就是你的问题。

sentences.en: You're vibe coding, which by definition literally means you're unwilling to look at the generated code.
sentences.zh: 您正在进行振动编码，根据定义，这意味着您不愿意查看生成的代码。

sentences.en: That's not what successful ai assisted software developers are doing.
sentences.zh: 这并不是成功的人工智能辅助软件开发人员正在做的事情。

sentences.en: YOU HAVE TO READ THE CODE.
sentences.zh: 您必须阅读代码。

sentences.en: Refusing to do that means you're not a serious programmer, you're outsourcing your thought and design and implementation, trying to get something for nothing by taking the easy way out, and you're going to get terrible results no matter what prompts you "engineer".
sentences.zh: 拒绝这样做意味着你不是一个认真的程序员，你正在外包你的思想、设计和实现，试图通过采取简单的方法来获得不劳而获的东西，并且无论什么促使你“工程师”，你都将得到可怕的结果。

sentences.en: There ain't no such thing as a free lunch (yet).And while we're at it, to elaborate on what serf said: people mindlessly parroting terms like "stochastic parrot" to criticize llms without having read the actual paper that coined the term and understanding what it really claimed and how other papers responded to it means you're just a human stochastic parrot no better than what you're criticizing -- at least the llm has read all those papers and understands what "stochastic parrot" actually means in context.
sentences.zh: 天下没有免费的午餐（还）。在我们讨论这个问题时，详细说明一下农奴所说的话：人们盲目地模仿“随机鹦鹉”这样的术语来批评LLM，而没有阅读创造该术语的实际论文并理解它真正声称的内容以及其他论文如何回应它，这意味着你只是一只人类随机鹦鹉，并不比你所批评的更好——至少LLM已经阅读了所有这些论文并理解了什么“随机鹦鹉”实际上是上下文中的意思。

sentences.en: Ask it, it will be glad to explain!Emily M.
sentences.zh: 询问它，它会很乐意解释！Emily M.

sentences.en: Bender, Timnit Gebru, Angelina McMillan-Major, and Margaret Mitchell.
sentences.zh: 本德、蒂姆尼特·格布鲁、安吉丽娜·麦克米伦·梅杰和玛格丽特·米切尔。

sentences.en: On the Dangers of Stochastic Parrots: Can Language Models Be Too Big?
sentences.zh: 关于随机鹦鹉学舌的危险：语言模型会太大吗？

blocks.html_en:
>It's now become so bad, I had to literally vibecode a separate linter for this.

You see, there's your problem right there. You're vibe coding, which by definition literally means you're unwilling to look at the generated code. That's not what successful ai assisted software developers are doing. YOU HAVE TO READ THE CODE. Refusing to do that means you're not a serious programmer, you're outsourcing your thought and design and implementation, trying to get something for nothing by taking the easy way out, and you're going to get terrible results no matter what prompts you "engineer". There ain't no such thing as a free lunch (yet).

And while we're at it, to elaborate on what serf said: people mindlessly parroting terms like "stochastic parrot" to criticize llms without having read the actual paper that coined the term and understanding what it really claimed and how other papers responded to it means you're just a human stochastic parrot no better than what you're criticizing -- at least the llm has read all those papers and understands what "stochastic parrot" actually means in context. Ask it, it will be glad to explain!

Emily M. Bender, Timnit Gebru, Angelina McMillan-Major, and Margaret Mitchell. On the Dangers of Stochastic Parrots: Can Language Models Be Too Big? (FAccT 2021)

https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3442188.3445922

blocks.html_en:
If this is your problem, I hate to say it, but it’s a skill issue on your side. I didnt even start developing software until LLMs even though I know all the primitives quite well and have a strong grasp of architecture. ADHD, perfection, and focus got in the way. I’m writing a pretty complex application and I don’t have the problems you’re running into. I regularly have my code reviewed by professional devs to ensure I’m not just vibecoding into slop. I’m not. I also read my code regularly and do a lot of exercises and courses to keep learning as well.

So I dunno what to say, except it’s possible to write really solid code with LLMs.

sentences.en: If this is your problem, I hate to say it, but it’s a skill issue on your side.
sentences.zh: 如果这是你的问题，我不想这么说，但这是你这边的技能问题。

sentences.en: I didnt even start developing software until LLMs even though I know all the primitives quite well and have a strong grasp of architecture.
sentences.zh: 尽管我非常了解所有原语并且对架构有很强的掌握，但直到法学硕士我才开始开发软件。

sentences.en: ADHD, perfection, and focus got in the way.
sentences.zh: 多动症、完美和专注都成为了障碍。

sentences.en: I’m writing a pretty complex application and I don’t have the problems you’re running into.
sentences.zh: 我正在编写一个非常复杂的应用程序，但我没有遇到您遇到的问题。

sentences.en: I regularly have my code reviewed by professional devs to ensure I’m not just vibecoding into slop.
sentences.zh: 我定期让专业开发人员审查我的代码，以确保我不只是将振动编码搞得一团糟。

sentences.en: I’m not.
sentences.zh: 我不是。

sentences.en: I also read my code regularly and do a lot of exercises and courses to keep learning as well.So I dunno what to say, except it’s possible to write really solid code with LLMs.
sentences.zh: 我还定期阅读我的代码，并做大量练习和课程来不断学习。所以我不知道该说什么，除了可以用法学硕士编写真正可靠的代码。

blocks.html_en: Generating leads for new work, if you are a freelance. Automatically answering customer support emails, if you own a SaaS. Monitoring competitors' socials, websites, etc for new features you have to compete with. Monitoring updates on software you depend on for breaking change / deprecation announcements.

sentences.en: Generating leads for new work, if you are a freelance.
sentences.zh: 如果您是自由职业者，则可以为新工作创造潜在客户。

sentences.en: Automatically answering customer support emails, if you own a SaaS.
sentences.zh: 如果您拥有 SaaS，则自动回复客户支持电子邮件。

sentences.en: Monitoring competitors' socials, websites, etc for new features you have to compete with.
sentences.zh: 监控竞争对手的社交媒体、网站等，以获取您必须与之竞争的新功能。

sentences.en: Monitoring updates on software you depend on for breaking change / deprecation announcements.
sentences.zh: 监控您所依赖的软件更新，以发布重大变更/弃用公告。

blocks.html_en: This implies a long road to catch up. But seems to be that all one needs to do to be on the cutting edge is to integrate a few scripts that support a cutting edge model. Which you then converse with in natural language. So how higher will the bar to entry be five years from now?

sentences.en: This implies a long road to catch up.
sentences.zh: 这意味着追赶的路还很长。

sentences.en: But seems to be that all one needs to do to be on the cutting edge is to integrate a few scripts that support a cutting edge model.
sentences.zh: 但似乎要处于最前沿，人们所需要做的就是集成一些支持前沿模型的脚本。

sentences.en: Which you then converse with in natural language.
sentences.zh: 然后您可以用自然语言与之交谈。

sentences.en: So how higher will the bar to entry be five years from now?
sentences.zh: 那么五年后的进入门槛将会有多高呢？

blocks.html_en:
>claude is

How? I mean what could be the ultimate usefulness of Claude if not to make money, just like NFT but with extra steps. Most people isn't using Claude to make massive paradigm shift breakthrough discoveries. Nobody's curing cancer nor solving climate change. Probably the most common use case for llms it's just speed up the grind and make money. Like nfts.

blocks.html_en: If curing cancer or solving climate change is your definition of usefulness, almost anything humans do is useless, probably including your own profession. Though I don't want to speculate. Speculation is precisely why NFTs are stupid. Claude, on the other hand, if used effectively, can speed up people's work and increase productivity.

sentences.en: If curing cancer or solving climate change is your definition of usefulness, almost anything humans do is useless, probably including your own profession.
sentences.zh: 如果治愈癌症或解决气候变化是你对有用的定义，那么人类所做的几乎所有事情都是无用的，可能包括你自己的职业。

sentences.en: Though I don't want to speculate.
sentences.zh: 虽然我不想猜测。

sentences.en: Speculation is precisely why NFTs are stupid.
sentences.zh: 投机正是 NFT 愚蠢的原因。

sentences.en: Claude, on the other hand, if used effectively, can speed up people's work and increase productivity.
sentences.zh: 另一方面，克劳德如果有效使用，可以加快人们的工作速度并提高生产力。

blocks.html_en:
Eventually everyone will be without a job other than the Bros working at AI companies.

Or the bubble will implode and we see ourselves at the job queue anyway.

blocks.html_en: Yeah these guys will set up claude on their third pc after they realize their second pc keeps hallucinating out code nobody cares about or needs in hopes of staying relevant. I'll just keep coding by hand because I ACTUALLY ENJOY it. What a novel concept these days.

sentences.en: Yeah these guys will set up claude on their third pc after they realize their second pc keeps hallucinating out code nobody cares about or needs in hopes of staying relevant.
sentences.zh: 是的，这些人在意识到他们的第二台电脑不断幻觉出没人关心或需要的代码以保持相关性后，他们会在他们的第三台电脑上设置克劳德。

sentences.en: I'll just keep coding by hand because I ACTUALLY ENJOY it.
sentences.zh: 我会继续手工编码，因为我真的很喜欢它。

sentences.en: What a novel concept these days.
sentences.zh: 如今这是多么新颖的概念啊。

blocks.html_en: I currently have Claude Desktop installed on a separate Mac mini M4 and control it with Dispatch. Is there a reason to do this method, it still seems the way I have it setup it has full control over the local account I gave it on the Mac mini.

sentences.en: I currently have Claude Desktop installed on a separate Mac mini M4 and control it with Dispatch.
sentences.zh: 我目前在单独的 Mac mini M4 上安装了 Claude Desktop，并使用 Dispatch 对其进行控制。

sentences.en: Is there a reason to do this method, it still seems the way I have it setup it has full control over the local account I gave it on the Mac mini.
sentences.zh: 有没有理由这样做，它似乎仍然是我设置它的方式，它可以完全控制我在 Mac mini 上给它的本地帐户。

blocks.html_en: The gpu inference happens on anthropics side, but the output of the gpu inference is often many local tool calls. The agent is locally reading files, writing files, compiling programs, downloading artifacts, running tests, invoking CLIs, monitoring logs, inspecting web sites, grepping etc, and can be doing all of that at once, in parallel via subagents, much faster than a single human ever could.

sentences.en: The gpu inference happens on anthropics side, but the output of the gpu inference is often many local tool calls.
sentences.zh: GPU 推理发生在人类方面，但 GPU 推理的输出通常是许多本地工具调用。

sentences.en: The agent is locally reading files, writing files, compiling programs, downloading artifacts, running tests, invoking CLIs, monitoring logs, inspecting web sites, grepping etc, and can be doing all of that at once, in parallel via subagents, much faster than a single human ever could.
sentences.zh: 代理在本地读取文件、写入文件、编译程序、下载工件、运行测试、调用 CLI、监控日志、检查网站、grep 等，并且可以通过子代理并行地同时执行所有这些操作，速度比单个人快得多。

blocks.html_en:
I found Dispatch to be less capable than RC. The latter is more like the direct Code experience (though there are some gaps in RC mode vs in a Claude-owned sandbox).

I’d love to just have Claude use my machine as a sandbox host instead of having to run RC on each host session. (In case you are listening Boris ;) ).

In the meantime I have a janky master RC session that creates new tmux windows and Claude RC sessions for each new code trajectory that I want to run.

The other benefit here is you can drop down and use termux to use Code directly if you hit a RC bug, I found permissions UX to be a bit flaky in the iOS UI.

sentences.en: I found Dispatch to be less capable than RC.
sentences.zh: 我发现 Dispatch 的能力不如 RC。

sentences.en: The latter is more like the direct Code experience (though there are some gaps in RC mode vs in a Claude-owned sandbox).I’d love to just have Claude use my machine as a sandbox host instead of having to run RC on each host session.
sentences.zh: 后者更像是直接的代码体验（尽管 RC 模式与 Claude 拥有的沙箱中存在一些差距）。我希望 Claude 使用我的计算机作为沙箱主机，而不必在每个主机会话上运行 RC。

sentences.en: (In case you are listening Boris ;) ).In the meantime I have a janky master RC session that creates new tmux windows and Claude RC sessions for each new code trajectory that I want to run.The other benefit here is you can drop down and use termux to use Code directly if you hit a RC bug, I found permissions UX to be a bit flaky in the iOS UI.
sentences.zh: （如果你正在听 Boris 的话；））。与此同时，我有一个 janky master RC 会话，它为我想要运行的每个新代码轨迹创建新的 tmux 窗口和 Claude RC 会话。这里的另一个好处是，如果遇到 RC bug，你可以下拉并使用 termux 直接使用代码，我发现 iOS UI 中的权限 UX 有点不稳定。

blocks.html_en:
I found Dispatch to be less capable than RC. The latter is more like the direct Code experience (though there are some gaps in RC mode vs in a Claude-owned sandbox).

I’d love to just have Claude use my machine as a sandbox host instead of having to run RC on each host session. (In case you are listening Boris ;) ).

In the meantime I have a janky master RC session that creates new tmux windows and Claude RC sessions for each new code trajectory that I want to run.

The other benefit here is you can drop down and use termux to use Code directly if you hit a RC bug, I found permissions UX to be a bit flaky in the iOS UI.

blocks.html_en: Dispatch/Cowork is basically claude code in a container. The section "Why not run it in a container?" would address your question in the post. One example I run into is that Cowork won't download and fill out or read pdfs or other files due to container permissions. Vanilla claude code has no problem using curl and wget.

blocks.html_en:
Cowork gets tangled with git as well. Fails, and then can't delete lock files.

Running a helper from the terminal, making Claude work in a working directory, and then create a .commit file has been my workaround for this for a while now.

Imagine there's a better solution nowadays, but this allows me to use dispatch building on Vercel, so I can check it out from wherever, without too much pain.

sentences.en: Cowork gets tangled with git as well.
sentences.zh: Cowork 也与 git 纠缠在一起。

sentences.en: Fails, and then can't delete lock files.Running a helper from the terminal, making Claude work in a working directory, and then create a .commit file has been my workaround for this for a while now.Imagine there's a better solution nowadays, but this allows me to use dispatch building on Vercel, so I can check it out from wherever, without too much pain.
sentences.zh: 失败，然后无法删除锁定文件。从终端运行帮助程序，让 Claude 在工作目录中工作，然后创建一个 .commit 文件，这一直是我的解决方法。想象一下现在有一个更好的解决方案，但这允许我在 Vercel 上使用调度构建，这样我就可以从任何地方查看它，而不会太痛苦。

blocks.html_en:
Claude dispatch does things differently than this. Dispatch is very convenient for somethings (including connectors).

But increasingly it seems like dispatch was slapped on top of cowork incrementally, when there was not an integrated and cohesive strategy across cowork between mobile/desktop/laptop. This is kind of what many of us get/got to learn in our 20's.

sentences.en: Claude dispatch does things differently than this.
sentences.zh: 克劳德派遣的做法与此不同。

sentences.en: Dispatch is very convenient for somethings (including connectors).But increasingly it seems like dispatch was slapped on top of cowork incrementally, when there was not an integrated and cohesive strategy across cowork between mobile/desktop/laptop.
sentences.zh: 调度对于某些东西（包括连接器）来说非常方便。但是，当移动/桌面/笔记本电脑之间的协同工作没有集成且有凝聚力的策略时，调度似乎逐渐被逐渐地置于协同工作之上。

sentences.en: This is kind of what many of us get/got to learn in our 20's.
sentences.zh: 这是我们许多人在 20 多岁的时候需要学习的。

blocks.html_en: Though it doesn't get by all the hurdles mentioned, it is alternatively possible to run Mac OS in a VM on your Mac using UTM and install Claude Code within the UTM VM. UTM can be run under a non-admin Mac account. This can allow you to use most Mac-native tooling, at least. The interactive performance of using the Claude Code ui on the VM isn't great, however. I'm not sure if you can log into the VM via terminal on the host from the non-root admin account to avoid the ui performance issues.

sentences.en: Though it doesn't get by all the hurdles mentioned, it is alternatively possible to run Mac OS in a VM on your Mac using UTM and install Claude Code within the UTM VM.
sentences.zh: 尽管它没有克服提到的所有障碍，但也可以使用 UTM 在 Mac 上的虚拟机中运行 Mac 操作系统，并在 UTM 虚拟机中安装 Claude Code。

sentences.en: UTM can be run under a non-admin Mac account.
sentences.zh: UTM 可以在非管理员 Mac 帐户下运行。

sentences.en: This can allow you to use most Mac-native tooling, at least.
sentences.zh: 这至少可以让您使用大多数 Mac 原生工具。

sentences.en: The interactive performance of using the Claude Code ui on the VM isn't great, however.
sentences.zh: 然而，在虚拟机上使用 Claude Code ui 的交互性能并不是很好。

sentences.en: I'm not sure if you can log into the VM via terminal on the host from the non-root admin account to avoid the ui performance issues.
sentences.zh: 我不确定您是否可以通过主机上的终端从非根管理员帐户登录虚拟机以避免用户界面性能问题。

blocks.html_en: An argument against this method is UTM doesn't support graphics acceleration so browser support will be hobbled. Even if you don't need the acceleration, I've found browsers in UTM virtualized OS's can't get past some modern captchas and other browser fingerprinting checkpoints :\. It's terrible but that's the way it is.

sentences.en: An argument against this method is UTM doesn't support graphics acceleration so browser support will be hobbled.
sentences.zh: 反对此方法的一个论点是 UTM 不支持图形加速，因此浏览器支持将受到阻碍。

sentences.en: Even if you don't need the acceleration, I've found browsers in UTM virtualized OS's can't get past some modern captchas and other browser fingerprinting checkpoints :\.
sentences.zh: 即使您不需要加速，我也发现 UTM 虚拟化操作系统中的浏览器无法通过一些现代验证码和其他浏览器指纹检查点：\。

sentences.en: It's terrible but that's the way it is.
sentences.zh: 这很可怕，但事实就是如此。

> browsers in UTM virtualized OS's can't get past some modern captchas and other browser fingerprinting checkpoints :\

I'm using Qubes OS, where everything runs in VMs without GPU acceleration, and never experienced this.

blocks.html_en: I've done this before as a free replacement for my OpenClaw bot. It loses its connection sometimes and you need to redo the handoff with your phone. But otherwise, nice to have a use for my old Mac. It's also running my Home Bridge, which Claude can interact with. Pretty cool.

blocks.html_en: I just redid my homelab/media server (switched from an old NUC I couldn’t figure out how to stop overheating even with some decent modification work, now using an hp elitedesk with an i5 processor that is handling my stack nicely). Thinking about setting up a vm on the base ubuntu install for isolation to run Claude in. May play with dispatch, may just put raw Claude code then use Moshi app on my iPhone and iPad.

sentences.en: I just redid my homelab/media server (switched from an old NUC I couldn’t figure out how to stop overheating even with some decent modification work, now using an hp elitedesk with an i5 processor that is handling my stack nicely).
sentences.zh: 我刚刚重新设计了我的家庭实验室/媒体服务器（从旧的 NUC 切换而来，即使做了一些像样的修改工作，我也不知道如何停止过热，现在使用带有 i5 处理器的 hp Elitedesk，可以很好地处理我的堆栈）。

sentences.en: Thinking about setting up a vm on the base ubuntu install for isolation to run Claude in.
sentences.zh: 考虑在基本的 ubuntu 安装上设置一个虚拟机以隔离运行 Claude。

sentences.zh: 可以玩调度，可能只需输入原始 Claude 代码，然后在我的 iPhone 和 iPad 上使用 Moshi 应用程序。

I’ve been doing something similar with an old m2. It isn’t powerful enough for local models, well sufficient local models but for openclaw and Claude it’s been perfect.

MacBook m1/m2 also are cheap enough now vs an Mac mini which I was surprised about, not too surprised but yeah..

blocks.html_en:
I agree, and it's not necessary for agents to run as root and full access to data.

There has always been a trade off sold to consumers of security vs convenience and a belief that giving up a little security gets a lot of convenience.

It ends up being often about a convenience of adopting the new tech, not necessarily in a way that's in the best long term interests of each party.

sentences.en: I agree, and it's not necessary for agents to run as root and full access to data.There has always been a trade off sold to consumers of security vs convenience and a belief that giving up a little security gets a lot of convenience.It ends up being often about a convenience of adopting the new tech, not necessarily in a way that's in the best long term interests of each party.
sentences.zh: 我同意，代理没有必要以 root 身份运行并完全访问数据。一直以来，消费者都需要在安全性与便利性之间进行权衡，并且相信放弃一点安全性会得到很多便利。最终往往是为了方便采用新技术，而不一定是为了符合各方的最佳长期利益。

blocks.html_en: Giving sudo permissions to an agent seems reckless. Claude gets his own unprivileged UNIX account, no more. I don't bother with containers or VMs however.

sentences.en: Giving sudo permissions to an agent seems reckless.
sentences.zh: 向代理授予 sudo 权限似乎很鲁莽。

sentences.en: Claude gets his own unprivileged UNIX account, no more.
sentences.zh: Claude 获得了他自己的非特权 UNIX 帐户，仅此而已。

sentences.en: I don't bother with containers or VMs however.
sentences.zh: 不过，我并不关心容器或虚拟机。

blocks.html_en:
It's so strange to me that tech crowd uses macs for use cases that spend all their time idle or serve some light http request.

It's a glorified API gateway...it could run on a medium sized potato

Almost like an entire generation just grew up coding on macbooks as the obvious choice for coders and just can't conceptualize hardware outside of that walled garden

sentences.en: It's so strange to me that tech crowd uses macs for use cases that spend all their time idle or serve some light http request.It's a glorified API gateway...it could run on a medium sized potatoAlmost like an entire generation just grew up coding on macbooks as the obvious choice for coders and just can't conceptualize hardware outside of that walled garden
sentences.zh: 对我来说，技术人群使用 mac 来处理那些花费所有时间闲置或服务一些轻型 http 请求的用例真是太奇怪了。这是一个美化的 API 网关...它可以在一个中等大小的土豆上运行几乎就像整整一代人都在 macbook 上编码一样，作为程序员的明显选择，只是无法概念化围墙花园之外的硬件

blocks.html_en:
I've been putting off learning Claude and this article had me strongly considering jumping in. Then I looked up Anthropic pricing and its 100x more convoluted than cloud services management. Its a goddamn full time job and independent skill set figuring out how to prevent going bankrupt from AI usage!

I think I'm gonna be a late adopter on this one until the industry figures out a less cumbersome pricing model.

blocks.html_en:
Look at the subscriptions. That's how anyone using the stuff as an individual should be approaching it today if they are dealing directly with Anthropic or OpenAI.

With the $200 Claude subscription I was able to get around $13-15k of API equivalent usage in one month (note: this was during the "+50% usage" promotion that they have kept extending since May). When you hit your usage limit for a given time period you get cut off until the time period resets; don't bother paying for additional usage credits, you will be disappointed.

sentences.en: Look at the subscriptions.
sentences.zh: 看看订阅情况。

sentences.en: That's how anyone using the stuff as an individual should be approaching it today if they are dealing directly with Anthropic or OpenAI.With the $200 Claude subscription I was able to get around $13-15k of API equivalent usage in one month (note: this was during the "+50% usage" promotion that they have kept extending since May).
sentences.zh: 这就是任何以个人身份使用这些东西的人今天应该如何处理它，如果他们直接与 Anthropic 或 OpenAI 打交道。通过 200 美元的 Claude 订阅，我能够在一个月内获得大约 13-15k 美元的 API 等价使用量（注意：这是在他们自 5 月份以来不断延长的“+50% 使用率”促销期间）。

sentences.en: When you hit your usage limit for a given time period you get cut off until the time period resets; don't bother paying for additional usage credits, you will be disappointed.
sentences.zh: 当您在给定时间段内达到使用限制时，您将被切断，直到该时间段重置为止；不要费心支付额外的使用积分，你会失望的。

blocks.html_en: What I’m reading is this use case is for 3-5 days a week full time dev if you stick to off hours US time. That you can save significantly if you have spiky usage 1-2 days a week by going the ad hoc API route if you’re new and you need to install a bunch of monitoring tools to tell you how best to save week to eeek as your usage patterns change and you risk surprise bills in the 10s of thousands if you get it wrong.

sentences.en: What I’m reading is this use case is for 3-5 days a week full time dev if you stick to off hours US time.
sentences.zh: 我读到的是，如果您遵守美国时间的非工作时间，则此用例适用于每周 3-5 天的全职开发人员。

sentences.en: That you can save significantly if you have spiky usage 1-2 days a week by going the ad hoc API route if you’re new and you need to install a bunch of monitoring tools to tell you how best to save week to eeek as your usage patterns change and you risk surprise bills in the 10s of thousands if you get it wrong.
sentences.zh: 如果您每周有 1-2 天的使用量激增，那么您可以通过使用临时 API 路线来节省大量费用，如果您是新手，并且需要安装一堆监控工具来告诉您如何最好地节省一周的时间，因为您的使用模式发生了变化，而且如果您弄错了，您可能会面临数十万的意外账单。

blocks.html_en:
The approach I took was to start with the $20 plan, then when it became clear it wasn't sufficient I upgraded to the $100 plan. The $20 plan didn't cover one night of coding. With the $100 plan I started bumping into the 5 hour usage limits after ramping up over a week or two.

My average API equivalent use is around $30-40/hr. I would just bite the bullet on a plan for one month, then use that to calibrate your expectations around usage and cost optimization. The plans are heavily subsidized.

sentences.en: The approach I took was to start with the $20 plan, then when it became clear it wasn't sufficient I upgraded to the $100 plan.
sentences.zh: 我采取的方法是从 20 美元的计划开始，然后当我发现这还不够时，我升级到 100 美元的计划。

sentences.en: The $20 plan didn't cover one night of coding.
sentences.zh: 20 美元的计划不包括一晚的编码时间。

sentences.en: With the $100 plan I started bumping into the 5 hour usage limits after ramping up over a week or two.My average API equivalent use is around $30-40/hr.
sentences.zh: 使用 100 美元的计划，在一两周后，我开始遇到 5 小时的使用限制。我的平均 API 等效使用量约为 30-40 美元/小时。

sentences.en: I would just bite the bullet on a plan for one month, then use that to calibrate your expectations around usage and cost optimization.
sentences.zh: 我会硬着头皮制定一个月的计划，然后用它来校准您对使用和成本优化的期望。

sentences.en: The plans are heavily subsidized.
sentences.zh: 这些计划得到了大量补贴。

blocks.html_en:
The approach I took was to start with the $20 plan, then when it became clear it wasn't sufficient I upgraded to the $100 plan. The $20 plan didn't cover one night of coding. With the $100 plan I started bumping into the 5 hour usage limits after ramping up over a week or two.

My average API equivalent use is around $30-40/hr. I would just bite the bullet on a plan for one month, then use that to calibrate your expectations around usage and cost optimization. The plans are heavily subsidized.

I still don't understand what these freaks are doing running these agents 24/7 on machines. What are they doing? Managing a todo list? You mean crossing items off as you complete them? Research tasks? To do what?

Never really get good answers. There is no killer app. Just bikeshedding.

blocks.html_en:
Swiping Tinder. It takes about 5000 matches to get a date. It’s easier to just automate it. It automatically adds dates to my calendar, all I have to do is show up. I get a summary of our chat history (well, what the agent wrote to her) in the notes section of the calendar entry and some pointers and talking points for the date.

Maybe I should have the agent also do a background check.

PS: This is a joke, but feel free to steal this idea.

sentences.en: Swiping Tinder.
sentences.zh: 滑动火种。

sentences.en: It takes about 5000 matches to get a date.
sentences.zh: 大约需要 5000 场比赛才能找到约会对象。

sentences.en: It’s easier to just automate it.
sentences.zh: 自动化更容易。

sentences.en: It automatically adds dates to my calendar, all I have to do is show up.
sentences.zh: 它会自动将日期添加到我的日历中，我所要做的就是出现。

sentences.en: I get a summary of our chat history (well, what the agent wrote to her) in the notes section of the calendar entry and some pointers and talking points for the date.Maybe I should have the agent also do a background check.PS: This is a joke, but feel free to steal this idea.
sentences.zh: 我在日历条目的注释部分中获得了我们的聊天历史记录的摘要（嗯，是代理写给她的内容）以及日期的一些提示和谈话要点。也许我应该让代理也进行背景调查。PS：这是一个笑话，但请随意窃取这个想法。

blocks.html_en: The future is agents chatting to each other on tinder and automating the initial getting to know you part. I can imagine that while that's going on, we could add like a little text chat box for the humans to chit chat with each other a bit and pass the time, before they can go on a date

blocks.html_en:
Someone apparently made this

https://github.com/Grigorij-Dudnik/TinderGPT

> TinderGPT automates the process of writing and arranging dates with girls on Tinder, enabling you to generate romantic meetings with almost zero effort. Your only role is to like the profiles that catch your eye. After that, TinderGPT comes into the play. It initiates a conversation with the girl, using details from her profile, continues by building an emotional bond and highlighting your attractive traits, and finishes by arranging a meeting and giving you a push-up on your phone with her number.

sentences.en: Someone apparently made thishttps://github.com/Grigorij-Dudnik/TinderGPT> TinderGPT automates the process of writing and arranging dates with girls on Tinder, enabling you to generate romantic meetings with almost zero effort.
sentences.zh: 显然有人做了这个 https://github.com/Grigorij-Dudnik/TinderGPT> TinderGPT 自动化了在 Tinder 上与女孩编写和安排约会的过程，使您能够几乎零努力地进行浪漫的会面。

sentences.en: Your only role is to like the profiles that catch your eye.
sentences.zh: 您唯一的作用就是喜欢吸引您眼球的个人资料。

sentences.en: After that, TinderGPT comes into the play.
sentences.zh: 之后，TinderGPT 登场。

sentences.en: It initiates a conversation with the girl, using details from her profile, continues by building an emotional bond and highlighting your attractive traits, and finishes by arranging a meeting and giving you a push-up on your phone with her number.
sentences.zh: 它首先使用女孩个人资料中的详细信息与女孩进行对话，然后建立情感纽带并突出您的吸引力特征，最后安排一次会面并通过她的电话号码给您做俯卧撑。

blocks.html_en: This is not really the same at all. You still have to swipe, the value add here is apparently “building an emotional bond”. Together with the explicit goal of “dates with girls”, this actually feels incredibly nefarious.

sentences.en: This is not really the same at all.
sentences.zh: 这根本不一样。

sentences.en: You still have to swipe, the value add here is apparently “building an emotional bond”.
sentences.zh: 你仍然需要刷卡，这里的附加值显然是“建立情感纽带”。

sentences.en: Together with the explicit goal of “dates with girls”, this actually feels incredibly nefarious.
sentences.zh: 再加上“和女孩约会”的明确目标，这实际上让人感觉非常邪恶。

blocks.html_en:
It seems the main use case is having Claude automatically write blogposts about how great using Claude is, then submit them wherever necessary.

There's lots of news about the billions AI companies spend on data center construction, but it feels like it's not even a fraction of the money they're spending on endless nonstop blogs about how great their app is at doing... things. Things that will never be defined.

blocks.html_en:
Exact same question as you. When the new ChatGPT app dropped it suggested to me to set up a task something like (paraphrased) “every Monday read my Gmail and Slack an make a summary and task list for the week”.

Why would I need an LLM to do this for me? That’s 5 minutes of work max, and doing it gets me in the flow of work again, to see what’s going on and needs to be done.

sentences.en: Exact same question as you.
sentences.zh: 和你一模一样的问题。

sentences.en: When the new ChatGPT app dropped it suggested to me to set up a task something like (paraphrased) “every Monday read my Gmail and Slack an make a summary and task list for the week”.Why would I need an LLM to do this for me?
sentences.zh: 当新的 ChatGPT 应用程序下降时，它建议我设置一个任务，例如（解释）“每周一阅读我的 Gmail 和 Slack，为本周做一个总结和任务列表”。为什么我需要法学硕士来为我做这件事？

sentences.en: That’s 5 minutes of work max, and doing it gets me in the flow of work again, to see what’s going on and needs to be done.
sentences.zh: 最多工作 5 分钟，这样做可以让我再次进入工作流程，看看发生了什么以及需要做什么。

blocks.html_en:
For a lot of folks summarizing a few days of work email and especially slack chats is way more than 5 minutes. Some work environments do not have great communication hygiene so it can be overwhelming to try to keep up with 500 emails a day and 38 Slack channels.

For the folks I talk to who use a LLM for this that seems to be the case. Takes a huge cognitive load off every morning and saves them an hour or two.

More or less a very expensive band aid over a bad work environment.

I kinda use it the same way in a sense. I have a little skill I run against our (horrible) task management system to summarize things and give me a punchlist to work through sorted by priority. This saves me thousands of clicks to do the same thing in the horrible web UI. A proper system in the first place would be a lot better!

At some point I’ll probably just take that to the next logical step and have the LLM write my own web interface to abstract and replace the horrible one entirely for me.

sentences.en: For a lot of folks summarizing a few days of work email and especially slack chats is way more than 5 minutes.
sentences.zh: 对于很多人来说，总结几天的工作电子邮件，尤其是闲聊的时间远远超过 5 分钟。

sentences.en: Some work environments do not have great communication hygiene so it can be overwhelming to try to keep up with 500 emails a day and 38 Slack channels.For the folks I talk to who use a LLM for this that seems to be the case.
sentences.zh: 有些工作环境没有良好的沟通卫生，因此尝试每天处理 500 封电子邮件和 38 个 Slack 频道可能会让人不知所措。对于我采访过的使用法学硕士的人来说，情况似乎就是这样。

sentences.en: Takes a huge cognitive load off every morning and saves them an hour or two.More or less a very expensive band aid over a bad work environment.I kinda use it the same way in a sense.
sentences.zh: 每天早上减轻巨大的认知负担，节省一两个小时。在恶劣的工作环境中或多或少是一个非常昂贵的创可贴。从某种意义上说，我也以同样的方式使用它。

sentences.en: I have a little skill I run against our (horrible) task management system to summarize things and give me a punchlist to work through sorted by priority.
sentences.zh: 我有一点技巧，可以在我们的（可怕的）任务管理系统中进行总结，并给我一个按优先级排序的工作清单。

sentences.en: This saves me thousands of clicks to do the same thing in the horrible web UI.
sentences.zh: 这节省了我在可怕的 Web UI 中执行相同操作的数千次点击。

sentences.en: A proper system in the first place would be a lot better!At some point I’ll probably just take that to the next logical step and have the LLM write my own web interface to abstract and replace the horrible one entirely for me.
sentences.zh: 首先，一个合适的系统会好得多！在某些时候，我可能会将其带到下一个逻辑步骤，让法学硕士编写我自己的 Web 界面，以完全为我抽象和替换可怕的界面。

blocks.html_en: Because then OpenAI can read your emails and project communications and eventually build a model they will sell as an automated consultant. The CEOs will uncritically eat it up just long enough to cut the footing out from the industry. Once everyone is used to the sorry state of software, nobody will be able to imagine putting people to the task anymore and we'll have the new world order that Altman and Theil have been talking about creating.

sentences.en: Because then OpenAI can read your emails and project communications and eventually build a model they will sell as an automated consultant.
sentences.zh: 因为这样 OpenAI 就可以读取您的电子邮件和项目通信，并最终构建一个模型，他们将作为自动化顾问出售。

sentences.en: The CEOs will uncritically eat it up just long enough to cut the footing out from the industry.
sentences.zh: 首席执行官们会不加批判地消化它，直到切断该行业的立足点。

sentences.en: Once everyone is used to the sorry state of software, nobody will be able to imagine putting people to the task anymore and we'll have the new world order that Altman and Theil have been talking about creating.
sentences.zh: 一旦每个人都习惯了软件的糟糕状态，就没有人能够想象再让人们承担这项任务，我们将拥有奥特曼和泰尔一直在谈论创建的新世界秩序。

I’m actually very time-poor, so figured it could help be clawed back time doing… what exactly?

blocks.html_en:
I think you need to open your mind to the possibilities? For example:

- scanning logs for errors and

- opening issues which are then auto-triaged and

- PRs are opened for them and auto-reviewed and

- merged (and deployed).

This workflow alone is immensely powerful, and takes alot of burden off the team.

blocks.html_en:
> This workflow alone is immensely powerful, and takes alot of burden off the team.

ITSM those unsupervised workflows are essentially an attempt at purported productivity in the near term at the expense of meaningful incremental long term burden for teams.

The only ostensible benefit is in the eyes of the AI-psychotic tinkerer, who knows no better, or in those of the clout-chasing developer farming likes on their LinkedIn posts.

sentences.en: > This workflow alone is immensely powerful, and takes alot of burden off the team.ITSM those unsupervised workflows are essentially an attempt at purported productivity in the near term at the expense of meaningful incremental long term burden for teams.The only ostensible benefit is in the eyes of the AI-psychotic tinkerer, who knows no better, or in those of the clout-chasing developer farming likes on their LinkedIn posts.
sentences.zh: > 这个工作流程本身就非常强大，并减轻了团队的很多负担。ITSM 那些无监督的工作流程本质上是为了在短期内提高所谓的生产力，但代价是为团队带来有意义的增量长期负担。唯一表面上的好处是在人工智能精神病修补者的眼中，他们不知道更好，或者在那些追逐影响力的开发人员在他们的 LinkedIn 帖子上点赞的眼中。

blocks.html_en:
Why are the errors occurring, though? That's what boring analyse-and-fix addresses, through familiarity, recognition of patterns and "hang onnn..." moments.

It's like your AI agent is just plugging the leaks in the dyke each time, instead of fixing the architecture of the dam.

sentences.en: Why are the errors occurring, though?
sentences.zh: 但为什么会出现错误呢？

sentences.en: That's what boring analyse-and-fix addresses, through familiarity, recognition of patterns and "hang onnn..."
sentences.zh: 这就是无聊的分析和修复所要解决的问题，通过熟悉、识别模式和“坚持下去……”

sentences.en: moments.It's like your AI agent is just plugging the leaks in the dyke each time, instead of fixing the architecture of the dam.
sentences.zh: 就像你的人工智能代理每次只是堵住堤坝的漏洞，而不是修复大坝的结构。

blocks.html_en:
Yes, I want my team to be deeply familiar with the codebase and every single little bug that needs fixing both trains them and let's them learn a little bit more about the codebase.

They can use agents. Like, team members don't need to be replaced, they can simply use agents when they deem it useful. If they see a trivial bug,they can put their agent on it and go work on something else meanwhile.

sentences.en: Yes, I want my team to be deeply familiar with the codebase and every single little bug that needs fixing both trains them and let's them learn a little bit more about the codebase.They can use agents.
sentences.zh: 是的，我希望我的团队能够深入熟悉代码库，每一个需要修复的小错误都会对他们进行培训，让他们更多地了解代码库。他们可以使用代理。

sentences.en: Like, team members don't need to be replaced, they can simply use agents when they deem it useful.
sentences.zh: 比如，团队成员不需要更换，他们可以简单地在他们认为有用的时候使用代理。

sentences.en: If they see a trivial bug,they can put their agent on it and go work on something else meanwhile.
sentences.zh: 如果他们发现一个小错误，他们可以让他们的代理处理它，同时继续处理其他事情。

blocks.html_en:
Let me guess -- in your day job you don't manage people. I have agents parsing messages, building out document sets, evaluating existing document sets, one is currently fixing a giant backlog of bugs and feature requests for a multi year personal coding project, one is exploring some ideas on speeding up inference at the edge..

If you put yourself in a position where you need more leverage (technical or operating) I think you might find you get some value.

sentences.en: Let me guess -- in your day job you don't manage people.
sentences.zh: 让我猜猜——在你的日常工作中你不管理人员。

sentences.en: I have agents parsing messages, building out document sets, evaluating existing document sets, one is currently fixing a giant backlog of bugs and feature requests for a multi year personal coding project, one is exploring some ideas on speeding up inference at the edge..If you put yourself in a position where you need more leverage (technical or operating) I think you might find you get some value.
sentences.zh: 我有代理解析消息、构建文档集、评估现有文档集，其中一个正在修复一个多年个人编码项目的大量积压的错误和功能请求，一个正在探索一些加速边缘推理的想法。如果您将自己置于需要更多杠杆（技术或操作）的位置，我认为您可能会发现自己获得了一些价值。

blocks.html_en:
No sarcasm, I am completely serious.

I don’t have time for much leisure coding these days. I do have time to kick off a few tasks in the morning to progress my many side projects. Nothing public / oss, just code that I find useful/interesting like home automation, content pipelines, games, etc.

There are a bunch of cases where remote control from iOS onto a Mac Mini is simply nicer than using iOS Claude Code sandboxes.

It’s the same pattern as you (hopefully) apply at $dayjob. If you are not defining a /goal and letting your agent crank you are not making full use of the models’ capabilities.

sentences.en: No sarcasm, I am completely serious.I don’t have time for much leisure coding these days.
sentences.zh: 没有讽刺，我是完全认真的。这些天我没有太多休闲编码的时间。

sentences.en: I do have time to kick off a few tasks in the morning to progress my many side projects.
sentences.zh: 我确实有时间在早上开始一些任务来推进我的许多副项目。

sentences.en: Nothing public / oss, just code that I find useful/interesting like home automation, content pipelines, games, etc.There are a bunch of cases where remote control from iOS onto a Mac Mini is simply nicer than using iOS Claude Code sandboxes.It’s the same pattern as you (hopefully) apply at $dayjob.
sentences.zh: 没有任何公开/oss，只是我觉得有用/有趣的代码，例如家庭自动化、内容管道、游戏等。在很多情况下，从 iOS 到 Mac Mini 的远程控制比使用 iOS Claude Code 沙箱更好。这与您（希望）在 $dayjob 中应用的模式相同。

sentences.en: If you are not defining a /goal and letting your agent crank you are not making full use of the models’ capabilities.
sentences.zh: 如果您没有定义/目标并让您的代理启动，那么您就没有充分利用模型的功能。

blocks.html_en:
Well I am fully of the opinion that LLMs can help in software programming, it's not something that I feel provides any value unless it has a human in the loop. The overhead of having to figure out if the agent did a good job, if the agent is actually done or not, and if the thing it built is shit or not, is worth simply avoiding by having a human in the loop.

So I wouldn't agree that the agent should be cranking out code all the time, in fact that seems more like a waste of resources compared to the work it creates. But I do understand home automation software can be very one-off and simple. But then again, a properly programmed home automation suite doesn't need a SOTA model to modify it, I think.

sentences.en: Well I am fully of the opinion that LLMs can help in software programming, it's not something that I feel provides any value unless it has a human in the loop.
sentences.zh: 我完全相信法学硕士可以帮助软件编程，但我认为除非有人参与其中，否则它不会提供任何价值。

sentences.en: The overhead of having to figure out if the agent did a good job, if the agent is actually done or not, and if the thing it built is shit or not, is worth simply avoiding by having a human in the loop.So I wouldn't agree that the agent should be cranking out code all the time, in fact that seems more like a waste of resources compared to the work it creates.
sentences.zh: 必须弄清楚代理是否做得很好，代理是否真正完成，以及它构建的东西是否糟糕，这些开销值得通过让人类参与循环来简单地避免。所以我不同意代理应该一直编写代码，事实上，与它创建的工作相比，这似乎更像是资源的浪费。

sentences.en: But I do understand home automation software can be very one-off and simple.
sentences.zh: 但我确实了解家庭自动化软件可以非常一次性且简单。

sentences.en: But then again, a properly programmed home automation suite doesn't need a SOTA model to modify it, I think.
sentences.zh: 但话又说回来，我认为，正确编程的家庭自动化套件不需要 SOTA 模型来修改它。

blocks.html_en:
On all projects I've run any of the models they:

- infinitely duplicate any and all code, helpers and components

- infinitely duplicate CSS (because they duplicate components)

- continuously write code like "read the entire db into memory and run a filter function on retrieved data"

- continuously write code like "call db with multiple queries for each element in a list"

- etc. etc.

Why the hell would I ever want to run them unsupervised?

sentences.en: On all projects I've run any of the models they:- infinitely duplicate any and all code, helpers and components- infinitely duplicate CSS (because they duplicate components)- continuously write code like "read the entire db into memory and run a filter function on retrieved data"- continuously write code like "call db with multiple queries for each element in a list"- etc. etc.Why the hell would I ever want to run them unsupervised?
sentences.zh: 在我运行过的所有项目中，它们： - 无限复制任何和所有代码、帮助程序和组件 - 无限复制CSS（因为它们复制组件） - 连续编写代码，例如“将整个数据库读入内存并对检索到的数据运行过滤函数” - 连续编写代码，例如“对列表中的每个元素进行多个查询来调用数据库” - 等等。为什么我会想在无人监督的情况下运行它们？

content_html_zh: 除了本文提到的图形开发之外，没有理由使用实际硬件来隔离代理。 我使用 libvirt 将这个脚本[0]组合在一起，为 claude 提供了自己的图形桌面环境，以便能够使用 Chrome 进行用户验收测试。 它有完整的根，可以做任何事情。 如果它弄乱了，我可以在几秒钟内转储并重新安装。0：https://gist.github.com/smith153/04b4068b5a2d7b234f1c3d5992d...好的。 我只是有 sudo useradd agent sudo su agent 所以它可以炸毁自己的文件，但不能炸毁我的文件。它还在里面做某种无头 Chrome 的东西。 我不知道它是如何工作的，但它正在截屏iirc。我也曾在某个时候设置过VNC，但发现它不值得使用。>如果它弄乱了，我可以在几秒钟内转储并重新安装。这对于3美元的VPS也是如此，我发现给我的代理root权限非常有趣。 可能发生的最坏的情况是什么；）很高兴给克劳德自己的蓝色气泡 iMessage。 当然，如果你有能力的话，可以在虚拟机中完成，但旧的苹果硬件似乎积累了很多。这太棒了，谢谢你的分享！我一直想建立一个完全像这样的东西供我自己使用，但是...... 你知道，时间有限。这只是足够周一早上做一个小项目的脚手架！Linux 上有很多方法和工具可以启动并运行“VM”。 但使用 libvirt，您可以轻松编写初始环境和网络堆栈的脚本。 它的核心只是运行“virt-install”命令，但正如你所看到的，在此之前我还做了很多其他固执己见的事情。你的意思是你让克劳德拼凑了一个脚本，因为这是大量生成的。 读了大约 250 行后就失去了兴趣，这种不连贯的融合读起来很无聊我不知道您每天必须检查哪种 shell 脚本... 当然，我曾经得到过更糟糕的 shell 脚本我只是无法想出一个好的 AI-is-actually-24/7-helping-me-out 用例。请帮忙：我需要这个！许多 Claude Code 高级用户不再真正使用 IDE，因此他们使用笔记本电脑而不是手机工作的唯一目的是因为这是正常的工作方式。这是一个真实的用例：您负责某些警报通道。 您已连接 datadog/cloudlogging/github。 当你外出时，你看到一堆警报出现，你提示 CC 进行调查 - Claude 进行分类并说“突然间，你从与你公司合作的银行 API 中得到了超时，这在一小时前就开始了。 约 15% 的请求都会发生这种情况”。 因此，您联系公司中负责供应商关系的人员，然后回到周末。这不是一个假设的示例。 显然，如果您的工作有真正的随叫随到轮换和更强大的警报，并且您不会在周末收到松弛警报，那就更好了…… 但我认为这份工作给了我很大的灵活性，所以没关系您没有随叫随到的轮换，但有专门负责供应商关系的人员，并且那个人在周末工作？ 我不确定您如何完全避免在周末收到有关第三方支付处理器问题的警报，如果您确实想在周末处理业务，这种情况随时可能发生。> 你们没有值班人员轮换，但有专人负责供应商关系，而那个人周末工作？我是客户经理。 如果我的客户认为值班开发人员尚未考虑问题，他们几乎会在任何时间（包括周末）打电话。我在这里说的是供应商，但它更像是我们合作的银行。 所以有人负责银行关系的技术方面。但是，是的，这是一个大多数周末都没有问题的区域，所以这不是一个重要的优先事项...... 直到它是不知怎的，在周末工作的银行联系人比随机的供应商更难以相信我不知道 GP 到底是做什么的，但“银行”涵盖了很多领域，而且有些事情发生在周末。 例如，“开放银行”API 和一些支付方式。> 许多 Claude Code 高级用户不再真正使用 IDE，这是我们 HN 泡沫中认为理所当然的事情。 我们大多数人都知道如何快速打字、使用编辑器、使用宏、程序脚本语言以及编写正则表达式。绝大多数程序员不知道这些事情。 因此，人工智能极大地加快了速度。是的，当你所做的只是在手机屏幕上输入一些自然语言时，一旦泡沫破灭，三年后你就能找到工作，祝你好运。 人们为不再使用 IDE 感到自豪对我来说是一个陌生的概念，我喜欢编码并因为热爱而进入这个行业。此类问题的“调试”是查看一些日志和 http 响应，然后“啊，如果我们收到此错误，则意味着他们再次重新启动防火墙并将我们从白名单中删除。 给银行的那个人乔发邮件，希望他回复”。 这不是火箭科学，也不是我的大部分工作…… 但有人需要这样做。 我们将所有能做的事情自动化。我不知道。晚上我一直在 Hulu 上看《它是如何制作的》才能入睡。尽管有自动化的能力，但我总是惊讶于有多少东西是用人手制作的。我运行了大量数据科学类型的分析，每次运行可能需要几个小时，因此克劳德大部分时间都在“监控”任务。 我可以远程控制它，这样当任务完成或需要澄清时我会收到通知，但最重要的是，每当我有新想法时，我都可以要求克劳德将其排队。 大多数时候我的硬件是瓶颈，而不是订阅配额。像这样的巧妙用例如此之多，以至于我对人工智能是互联网泡沫的反对者表示怀疑。 更不用说，与 2000 年不同的是，技术相关的人们现在可以快速地获得他们想法的合理近似值。我曾经有一些本地通知的钩子，但最近我发现克劳德非常擅长通过远程控制的应用程序进行通知（但绝对不完美）> 一次运行需要几个小时，所以 Claude 大部分时间都在“监视”任务 Claude 如何监视它们几个小时？ 即使根据 Anthropic 的说法，克劳德断章取义，而且极长的会话成本也高得令人望而却步（在他们放弃了长期运行任务的营销废话之后）？它在后台启动这些任务。 几个月前它变得非常擅长，现在它在计时器上设置监视器（不是我指示的，所以我假设它是此类任务的系统提示的一部分），然后只需等待下一个提示，完成后台进程，或等待监视器触发检查。> 然后等待下一个提示 根据 Anthropic 的说法，运行多个小时的单个会话的成本过高。 无论它只是等待提示还是执行某些操作。极其昂贵都是相对的。 在《寓言前》中，我对 1-2 个并发长时间运行任务加上交互式工作的 5x 计划感到满意（我为工作编写了大量代码，但这不是我的完整工作日）。 我认为 Opus 上每小时的缓存未命中不会造成太大影响，即使在 500-600k 的上下文中也是如此。 如果他们能够 /clear 进行远程控制工作，那就太好了。高额费用是否来自缓存未命中？ 如果他们的工作量确实需要等待很长时间才能继续，我想知道启动新会话并重新读取上下文和结果是否会更便宜。> 高额费用是否来自缓存未命中？是的。 他们无法将您的工作负载永远保留在缓存中，否则他们会耗尽用户的缓存。> 我想知道启动新会话并必须重新读取上下文和结果会更便宜。是的，这就是他们的建议当我需要销毁代币时，我将它用于以下用途： - 模糊测试，其目标是应用特定于领域和来源的知识来选择特定的模糊测试方法。 - 更一般地说，任何受益于特定领域或来源知情知识的优化问题。 - 运行 Microsoft 的 SkillOpt [0]。 [0]：https://github.com/microsoft/SkillOpt有一天，我使用 Claude Cowork 为我创建了一份清单，以便我可以安全地重新安装我的笔记本电脑...... 它经历了所有 ssh 配置、应用程序、下载、文档等...... 一旦我有了清单，我就要求它自行进行备份，以及在我重新安装笔记本电脑后重新安装/设置所有内容的说明。 这件事我已经拖延太久了……我自己发现很少有非常有用的案例1/使用 GUI 软件。 我的代理正在使用 Google Chrome 和 Figma。 拥有独立的环境很有帮助，不会干扰我的主机。2/运行长时间进程（1小时以上），因此我可以让主机关闭。3/运行密集型进程。 我用的是Gemma、Whisper和Qwen，它们会消耗主机CPU和资源。他们帮助固定费率计划的人们持续达到他们的使用限制，这让他们感觉钱花得值。让它控制浏览器并搜索一条给定尺寸、长度、颜色和款式的裤子。是的，令人惊讶的是，这是谷歌目前还无法做到的。我喜欢使用 /remote-control 来保持 Vibe 开发在我的使用限制和截止日期内顺利运行在“第二台 Mac”上的代码库上运行 Claude 代码 24/7，以便您可以在使用限制重置后始终从您的主设备或手机继续继续？我将所有智能扬声器更换为带有放大器和 pi 的旧收音机。 热词检测在 pi 本身上运行，但耳语和 LLM/任务编排则通过 4080 运行在我的本地服务器上。大约是 24/7。 更重要的是，当您的笔记本电脑放在包里并在运输途中，并且您已设置并想要运行某些功能时，它就无法工作。哦，是的，在空闲时间工作，以及在旅行时购买杂货养活自己，这样你就可以继续工作，这听起来很棒。否则那段时间就毫无价值了，所以是吗？我根本不珍惜我的旅行时间，但它曾经被浪费在旅行上。没有暗示或提及任何关于在空闲时间工作的事情，而是克劳德代码继续在任何事情上工作。这可能是为了个人项目或爱好。从你随身携带的计算机上独立运行进程会带来好处。我认为人工智能兄弟的主要用例是设置一个看起来傻乎乎的仪表板，将其命名为贾维斯，以扮演托尼斯塔克，并显示他们发布到社交媒体的所有生成视频的统计数据。（我希望我是在开玩笑）你是做软件开发的吗？ 一天结束时还有剩余工作吗？ 让它做那些事。白天我总是要纠正它的幻觉。 为什么我要让它在无人监管的情况下运行过夜？运行特工为自己的幻觉创建的测试？ 使用另一个幻觉代理进行优化？当所有代理不断做的主要事情之一是“从数据库加载所有记录，并在内存中过滤它们”时，您建议在经过一夜的监督工作后运行哪些测试和优化？ 现在它变得如此糟糕，我不得不为此编写一个单独的 linter。 这只是问题之一。我们没有可以在平庸的提示中创建出出色产品的人工智能大师。但我们确实有足够的人工智能，可以在出色的提示中制作出出色的产品。垃圾输入 -> 垃圾输出并没有消失。所以：就像任何盲目抱怨编译器讨厌他们的人一样：如果您确实需要帮助，请提供信息。 如果你只是想抱怨编译器很卑鄙，那就对天尖叫吧。很多人已经想出了如何让它工作；足以确认直接的 <gambling-machine>/<hallucinatory-psychopath>/<random-number-generator> 类比过于简单化，无法解释我们正在研究的内容。煽动。 你的“伟大的产品”（它在哪里？ 向我展示这些出色的产品）将具有极其低效的代码以及诸如“将整个数据库读入内存”之类的东西，无论提示如何。>很多人已经弄清楚如何让它发挥作用很多人声称他们已经弄清楚了。 事实上，这些人满嘴胡言乱语，并认为如果法学硕士能够开发出可用的软件，那就是很棒的可用软件。 并且还假设 LoC 是质量的衡量标准。因为所有模型都会继续这样做：https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48962703并且只有当您真正阅读代码并了解发生了什么时，您才能在“伟大的产品”中看到这一点。>现在变得如此糟糕，我不得不为此编写一个单独的 linter。你看，这就是你的问题。 您正在进行振动编码，根据定义，这意味着您不愿意查看生成的代码。 这并不是成功的人工智能辅助软件开发人员正在做的事情。 您必须阅读代码。 拒绝这样做意味着你不是一个认真的程序员，你正在外包你的思想、设计和实现，试图通过采取简单的方法来获得不劳而获的东西，并且无论什么促使你“工程师”，你都将得到可怕的结果。 天下没有免费的午餐（还）。在我们讨论这个问题时，详细说明一下农奴所说的话：人们盲目地模仿“随机鹦鹉”这样的术语来批评LLM，而没有阅读创造该术语的实际论文并理解它真正声称的内容以及其他论文如何回应它，这意味着你只是一只人类随机鹦鹉，并不比你所批评的更好——至少LLM已经阅读了所有这些论文并理解了什么“随机鹦鹉”实际上是上下文中的意思。 询问它，它会很乐意解释！Emily M. 本德、蒂姆尼特·格布鲁、安吉丽娜·麦克米伦·梅杰和玛格丽特·米切尔。 关于随机鹦鹉学舌的危险：语言模型会太大吗？ （FAccT 2021）https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3442188.3445922> 你看，你的问题就在那里。 您正在进行振动编码，根据定义，这意味着您不愿意查看生成的代码。 这并不是成功的人工智能辅助软件开发人员正在做的事情。 我有代理解析消息、构建文档集、评估现有文档集，其中一个正在修复一个多年个人编码项目的大量积压的错误和功能请求，一个正在探索一些加速边缘推理的想法。如果您将自己置于需要更多杠杆（技术或操作）的位置，我认为您可能会发现自己获得了一些价值。如果您无法想出足够的编码项目来让代理在后台忙碌，那么您这边就有技能问题了。我知道这可能是讽刺，但如果不是，你通过这种方式做副业能得到什么？没有讽刺，我是完全认真的。这些天我没有太多休闲编码的时间。 我确实有时间在早上开始一些任务来推进我的许多副项目。 没有任何公开/oss，只是我觉得有用/有趣的代码，例如家庭自动化、内容管道、游戏等。在很多情况下，从 iOS 到 Mac Mini 的远程控制比使用 iOS Claude Code 沙箱更好。这与您（希望）在 $dayjob 中应用的模式相同。 如果您没有定义/目标并让您的代理启动，那么您就没有充分利用模型的功能。我完全相信法学硕士可以帮助软件编程，但我认为除非有人参与其中，否则它不会提供任何价值。 必须弄清楚代理是否做得很好，代理是否真正完成，以及它构建的东西是否糟糕，这些开销值得通过让人类参与循环来简单地避免。所以我不同意代理应该一直编写代码，事实上，与它创建的工作相比，这似乎更像是资源的浪费。 但我确实了解家庭自动化软件可以非常一次性且简单。 但话又说回来，我认为，正确编程的家庭自动化套件不需要 SOTA 模型来修改它。在我运行过的所有项目中，它们： - 无限复制任何和所有代码、帮助程序和组件 - 无限复制CSS（因为它们复制组件） - 连续编写代码，例如“将整个数据库读入内存并对检索到的数据运行过滤函数” - 连续编写代码，例如“对列表中的每个元素进行多个查询来调用数据库” - 等等。为什么我会想在无人监督的情况下运行它们？有什么好办法可以给LLM有限的钱，比如2k或5k之类的。 但要让它与我的身份完全分开。就像我希望法学硕士有一个银行账户，他可以用这个银行账户做任何他想做的事情。 但他不能搞砸任何与我有关的事情。 他只有2 - 5k我不知道至少在美国你能怎么做。 我最想到的就是你可以为孩子们设置的支票账户之一。 它仍然会与你联系在一起。
